summary: The subject, Steve Vogon, performed unauthorized data exfiltration by copying sensitive files from an administrative share, compressing them, and using a Perl script to exfiltrate data via an external HTTP proxy. The activity included a deliberate, multi-stage attack chain: acquiring a privilege escalation exploit via 'wget' from Metasploit, executing it, and configuring an external HTTP proxy to camouflage exfiltration traffic as standard web traffic. Memory analysis confirms the link between the user (UID 501) and the exfiltrated data resident in RAM, providing definitive attribution and evidence of the exfiltration in progress.

artifacts.name: Unauthorized File Copy
artifacts.description: Mass copy of files from administrative share to personal directory detected in bash history.

artifacts.name: Exploit Acquisition and Execution
artifacts.description: Acquisition of privilege escalation exploit from Metasploit followed by execution, establishing intent for a multi-stage attack.

artifacts.name: Proxy Configuration and Camouflage
artifacts.description: Configuration of an external HTTP proxy to channel exfiltration traffic, specifically chosen to blend with standard web traffic to evade detection.

artifacts.name: Exfiltration Traffic
artifacts.description: Network traffic directed to an external proxy, camouflaged as normal web traffic.

artifacts.name: Anti-Forensics
artifacts.description: Deletion of the xfer.pl script after use to cover tracks.

artifacts.name: Memory Analysis Evidence
artifacts.description: Memory analysis confirms all suspicious strings map to user stevev (UID 501) and contains resident network packets with exfiltrated data, providing the definitive link between the actor and the exfiltration.
